evals.prompt: Can you search our internal RAGret knowledge base for information about the refund policy? I think we have an index called 'product_docs'.
evals.expected_output: Claude uses the RAGret skill to search the 'product_docs' index, runs the two-hop citation pipeline to download parent documents and extract line-level context, then answers with source citations including filename and line numbers.
expectations: The skill prompts for RAGRET_API_KEY and BASE_URL if not set, without asking for raw secrets in chat
The skill constructs the correct curl command to search the 'product_docs' index
The skill uses X-API-Key header with the API key from environment
The skill does not hardcode or expose the API key in plain text in commands shown to the user
The skill mentions parent_url, line_start, line_end as part of the search response
The skill runs the two-hop citation pipeline (download parent doc, read by line range)
The answer includes source citation with filename and line numbers

evals.prompt: I need to find something in our internal docs about the deployment process. Not sure what's available — can you check what indexes I have access to?
evals.expected_output: Claude first lists available indexes via the RAGret API, then asks which one to search, then performs the search with the two-hop citation pipeline.
expectations: The skill first lists indexes using the /api/subscribe-indexes endpoint
The skill presents the available indexes to the user before searching
The skill asks the user to choose an index or specify what they're looking for
The skill constructs the correct API URLs using BASE_URL environment variable
After searching, the skill downloads parent documents and extracts line-range context

evals.prompt: Search RAGret for 'microservices best practices' in the 'engineering' index and give me a summary with source citations.
evals.expected_output: Claude formulates the search query and retrieves results from the RAGret 'engineering' index, runs the two-hop pipeline to get full context from parent documents around matching lines, then summarizes the findings with source citations (filename + line numbers).
expectations: The skill uses the correct search endpoint /api/search/engineering
The skill passes the query as a URL-encoded parameter
The skill handles JSON response and extracts parent_url, line_start, line_end
The skill downloads parent documents to temp directory and reads relevant line ranges
The skill cites sources with filename and line numbers in the response

evals.prompt: I just got search results from RAGret and one of them has parent_url='/api/kb/engineering/parents/docs/architecture-guide.pdf.txt' with line_start=45 and line_end=67. Can you fetch the context around those lines so I can see the full picture?
evals.expected_output: Claude uses the parent command or direct curl to download the parent document and extracts lines 45-67, showing the expanded context to the user.
expectations: The skill constructs the full parent URL by combining BASE_URL with the parent_url path
The skill downloads the parent document to a local temp directory
The skill reads the specific line range (45-67) from the downloaded file
The skill handles the case where line_start/line_end might be null
